The PR updates sandbox bind validation to reject host bind sources that are parents of blocked system or credential paths and adds focused validator/create-args tests.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +36. Total +40 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current mainâ€™s validator checks source-inside-blocked only, and the existing test expected /var:/var to be allowed. I did not execute tests because this was a read-only review.

target.title: feat(tart): host-side WebVNC browser bridge for macOS leases
target.body:
## Summary

Makes `crabbox webvnc --provider tart` actually open a browser desktop viewer for macOS leases â€” the "just works" completion of the tart desktop work. The Linux direct path runs noVNC/`websockify` *on the guest*; macOS guests have neither, so this adds a **host-side bridge** that needs zero guest tooling.

> Stacked on #246 (tart `--desktop` / Screen Sharing). Review/merge #246 first; the diff here narrows to the bridge once #246 lands.

## How it works

`crabbox webvnc --provider tart --id <lease>`:
1. SSH-tunnels to the guest's native Screen Sharing (`127.0.0.1:5900`).
2. Starts a local HTTP server that serves the **embedded** noVNC viewer (vendored `rfb.js` + a small `vnc.html`) plus a `/websockify` WebSocket relayed to the tunnel (Go WebSocketâ†”TCP).
3. Opens the browser. noVNC authenticates via macOS **Apple (ARD) auth** with the lease account credentials.

The account credentials are handed to the **local viewer only** (a `127.0.0.1` URL) â€” never written to the guest or placed on a command line. A viewer-only `--tart-password` / `CRABBOX_TART_PASSWORD` (default `admin`, the cirruslabs base-image account password) supplies them. The `webvnc status`/`reset`/`daemon` subcommands (the Linux noVNC-daemon model) stay guarded and point to `crabbox webvnc`.

**Remote control plane (controller â†’ Mac â†’ guest):** run `crabbox webvnc` on the Mac and forward the printed web port to your machine â€” `ssh -L <port>:127.0.0.1:<port> <user>@<mac>` â€” then open the URL locally. (crabbox prints this hint.)

## Live proof â€” Apple Silicon M4 Pro, tart 2.32.1, macOS 26.4

```
$ crabbox warmup --provider tart --desktop --slug vncbr
provisioned ... state=ready

$ crabbox webvnc --provider tart --id cbx_...
bridge: serving noVNC locally; SSH tunnel -> guest 127.0.0.1:5900
webvnc: http://127.0.0.1:5902/vnc.html?password=admin&username=admin
remote: from another host first run  ssh -L 5902:127.0.0.1:5902 <user>@<this-host>  then open the URL there

# viewer served locally:
$ curl http://127.0.0.1:5902/vnc.html   -> "crabbox WebVNC", rfb.js, /websockify

# end-to-end relay (browser -> /websockify -> SSH tunnel -> guest Screen Sharing):
ws /websockify handshake: HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols
first relayed bytes: RFB 003.889          # the guest's VNC banner, through the bridge
```

A real noVNC browser then completes ARD auth (account creds) and renders the desktop.

## Files

| File | What |
|------|------|
| `internal/cli/macos_webvnc.go` | host-side bridge: tunnel + local HTTP/`/websockify` relay + browser |
| `internal/cli/webvnc_assets.go`, `internal/cli/webvncassets/*` | embedded noVNC viewer (`rfb.js` 1.7.0 + `vnc.html`) |
| `internal/cli/webvnc.go` | route macOS leases to the bridge; subcommand guard message |
| `internal/cli/config.go`, `internal/providers/tart/{flags,backend}.go` | viewer-only `--tart-password` |
| `internal/cli/macos_webvnc_test.go`, `docs/providers/tart.md` | tests + docs |

## Not included

- Resolution/scaling controls and multi-viewer slots (single foreground viewer).
- A daemonized background bridge for macOS (`webvnc daemon`).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
